name: Participant Recruitment Strategies
consequences: Immediate: Increased participant numbers → Systemic: Enhanced data quality and reliability → Strategic: Improved research outcomes and publication potential
options: Recruitment through specialist clinical interview only
Inclusion of collateral history from bed partner or household member
Targeted recruitment via social media and online forums
review: Controls participant numbers vs. data quality. Weakness: The options fail to consider the impact of participant compensation on recruitment rates.

name: Data Collection Methods
consequences: Immediate: Increased sensor data → Systemic: Enhanced event detection and triage → Strategic: Improved research outcomes and publication potential
options: Scheduled enhanced nights with fuller PSG montage
Escalation nights deploying full PSG after technician-flagged events
Continuous monitoring via dry-electrode EEG headbands and contact-free mattress sensors
review: Controls data quality vs. participant comfort. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential for sensor malfunction or false alarms.

name: Staffing and Training
consequences: Immediate: Safe residential monitoring operations → Systemic: Improved participant care and well-being → Strategic: Enhanced research outcomes and publication potential
options: Rotation of research technicians for night shifts
On-the-job training for new staff members
Hiring experienced sleep disorder specialists
review: Controls staffing ratios vs. event response times. Weakness: The options fail to consider the impact of staff turnover on data quality and participant care.

name: Budget Allocation
consequences: Immediate: Increased personnel costs → Systemic: Enhanced research capacity and outcomes → Strategic: Improved publication potential and grant funding
options: Priority allocation for equipment and staffing
Increased funding for participant compensation and data storage
Deferred expansion until pilot proves successful
review: Controls budget allocations vs. research priorities. Weakness: The options fail to consider the impact of budget constraints on research capacity and outcomes.

name: Governance and Review
consequences: Immediate: Enhanced external review and accountability → Systemic: Improved research governance and ethics compliance → Strategic: Enhanced publication potential and grant funding
options: Establishment of a scientific advisory board
Regular progress reviews with the DFG
Publication of interim results in peer-reviewed journals
review: Controls governance structures vs. research priorities. Weakness: The options fail to consider the impact of external review on participant recruitment and data collection.

name: Expansion and Intervention Studies
consequences: Immediate: Increased research capacity and outcomes → Systemic: Enhanced publication potential and grant funding → Strategic: Improved research impact and translation to clinical practice
options: Submission of a phase-two grant proposal
Establishment of a new research unit for REM sleep behavior disorder
Development of semi-automated event-triage tools
review: Controls expansion plans vs. research priorities. Weakness: The options fail to consider the impact of expansion on data quality and participant care.

name: Sensor Array Configurations
consequences: Immediate: Increased sensitivity → Systemic: Enhanced data quality → Strategic: Improved phenotyping capabilities.
options: Tiered acquisition model with escalating nights
Focal sensor placement for high-risk participants
Real-time signal processing and artifact rejection
review: Controls participant comfort vs. experimental rigor. Weakness: The options fail to consider the impact of participant fatigue on data quality.

name: Participant Engagement Strategies
consequences: Immediate: Improved compliance → Systemic: Enhanced data yield → Strategic: Increased research efficiency.
options: Personalized sleep diary and behavioral tracking
Gamified sleep education and monitoring
Real-time feedback and coaching on sleep hygiene
review: Controls participant burden vs. engagement. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential for participant manipulation.

name: Data Sharing Protocols
consequences: Immediate: Increased collaboration → Systemic: Enhanced research impact → Strategic: Improved funding opportunities.
options: Open-access de-identified physiological data
Restricted access to sensitive participant information
Conditional sharing of anonymized behavioral data
review: Controls intellectual property vs. public benefit. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential for data misuse.

name: Staffing and Training Models
consequences: Immediate: Improved safety → Systemic: Enhanced operational efficiency → Strategic: Increased research productivity.
options: Rotating night shifts with experienced technicians
Comprehensive training on sleep disorders and data analysis
Cross-training in multiple roles for staff flexibility
review: Controls staffing costs vs. expertise. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential for burnout among staff.

name: Budget Allocation Strategies
consequences: Immediate: Increased efficiency → Systemic: Enhanced financial sustainability → Strategic: Improved research competitiveness.
options: Prioritized funding for participant compensation and data analysis
Strategic investment in equipment upgrades and maintenance
Conditional allocation of funds based on research milestones
review: Controls costs vs. research goals. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential for cost overruns.

name: Governance and Review Frameworks
consequences: Immediate: Improved accountability → Systemic: Enhanced operational transparency → Strategic: Increased research credibility.
options: Regular external scientific advisory board reviews
Internal quality control checks with independent raters
Conditional funding contingent on meeting research milestones
review: Controls governance vs. autonomy. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential for conflicting priorities among stakeholders.

name: Residential Design Paradigms
consequences: Immediate: Increased participant comfort → Systemic: Reduced drop-out rates → Strategic: Enhanced longitudinal data quality
options: Domestic Revivalism
Mid-Century Modern Adaptation
Neuroscience-Inspired Architecture
review: Controls Participant Comfort vs. Research Efficiency. Weakness: The options fail to consider the impact of architectural style on parasomnia event capture.

name: Participant Screening Protocols
consequences: Immediate: Improved participant selection → Systemic: Enhanced data quality → Strategic: Increased research validity
options: Clinical Interview-Only
Structured Exclusion Checklist
Comprehensive Neurological Screening
review: Controls Participant Selection vs. Research Efficiency. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential benefits of machine learning-based participant selection algorithms.

name: Data Sharing Agreements
consequences: Immediate: Increased data accessibility → Systemic: Enhanced collaboration → Strategic: Accelerated research progress
options: De-identified Physiological Data Only
Full Anonymization Including Video Footage
Open Access to All Collected Data
review: Controls Research Collaboration vs. Participant Privacy. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential benefits of data sharing for participant recruitment.
